subject: Configure Expo for Multi-Platform Build
description: Update app.json with complete configuration: name, slug, version, orientation, icon, splash (backgroundColor matching theme), iOS (bundleIdentifier: com.worktracker.app, supportsTablet: true, infoPlist for Google Sign-In), android (package: com.worktracker.app, adaptiveIcon, intentFilters for OAuth), web (favicon, bundler: metro). Create eas.json with build profiles: development (development client), preview (internal distribution), production (store submission). Configure environment variables in eas.json (SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY). Add scripts to package.json: build:ios, build:android, build:web. Document EAS setup in docs/MOBILE_BUILD.md.
result_summary: Configured Expo multi-platform build support for WorkTracker. Updated app.json with explicit Google Sign-In URL scheme metadata and OAuth intent filters covering both worktracker://auth/callback and https://worktracker.app/auth/callback. Added app.config.js to inject SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, GOOGLE_IOS_REVERSED_CLIENT_ID, and EAS_PROJECT_ID into Expo config at build time without hardcoding secrets. Created eas.json with development, preview, and production build profiles bound to same-named EAS environments. Added docs/MOBILE_BUILD.md covering EAS setup, required environment variables, build profiles, and platform build commands. Verification: npm run typecheck passed; npx expo config --type public resolved correctly with sample env values; npm run build:web passed with sample env values. No new endpoints or external inputs were added, so input validation/auth/authz/error-path test items were not applicable to this task. Git commit created: [task-033] Configure Expo multi-platform build (b634d77). git pull --rebase was attempted before commit but failed because the shared worktree contained unrelated unstaged changes.
